data.title: [brainstorm/SC-generation]: unverifiable observable-behavior SCs created without prohibition -> sprint closes with open measurement gaps
data.description:
Actual Behavior: /dso:brainstorm generated SC13 for epic 736d-b957 as a top-level success criterion: 'workflow-restart rate drops ≥30% against pre-epic baseline.' This requires live production data accumulated over time after deployment. The completion-verifier correctly flagged it as FAIL because no measurement data exists at sprint close time.

Expected Behavior: /dso:brainstorm and the preplanning phase should prohibit or explicitly classify SCs that require post-deployment observable-behavior measurement. Such SCs cannot be verified during the sprint session and should be either: (a) moved to a separate post-deployment measurement task automatically, or (b) require the author to mark them as DEFERRED_MEASUREMENT with an explicit measurement plan.

Impact: Creates false failures at completion-verifier time, requiring manual intervention to create follow-up tickets and explain why the epic can still be closed. Erodes trust in the verification gate.

Proposed fix: Add a SC classification step in brainstorm/preplanning that identifies observable-behavior SCs (those requiring live production data, A/B test results, or time-series measurements) and either rejects them from the verifiable SC list or routes them to a post-deployment measurement story automatically.
